feedback_text:
DIAGNOSIS: The code demonstrates sophisticated CSV parsing capabilities but loses points primarily on missing test examples (1.8 points) and incomplete integration of advanced features. The main gaps are in test coverage and ensuring the advanced detection/generation capabilities work seamlessly without user configuration.

DO NOT CHANGE (these sections are passing):
  ✓ code_idiomaticness: Perfect score (8/8) - maintains excellent Python idioms and stdlib usage

REQUIRED FIXES (ordered by point impact):

  FIX 1: code_testability.test_examples (current: 0%, 1.8 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Includes example test cases? NO
    Expected: Test cases included for 1.0, doctest for 0.5, or none for 0.0
    Found: not found in content
    → ACTION: Add a comprehensive test section after the main function with at least 3 test cases: (1) basic CSV parsing with comma delimiter, (2) edge case with missing headers and tab delimiter, (3) error handling test with malformed data. Include assertions and expected outputs for each test case.

  FIX 2: code_correctness.delimiter_detection (current: 75%, 1.1 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Missing full auto-detection capability
    Expected: 1.0 if auto-detects from data, 0.5 if parameterized, 0.0 if hardcoded
    Found: Advanced detection present but may require parameters
    → ACTION: Modify the main parse_csv function to automatically call detect_delimiter() when no delimiter is specified in config, ensuring the function can process any CSV file without requiring delimiter parameters from the user.

  FIX 3: code_correctness.header_handling (current: 75%, 1.1 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Missing seamless auto-detection + generation integration
    Expected: 1.0 if auto-detects + generates, 0.5 if one or other, 0.0 if assumes headers
    Found: Both detection and generation functions exist but integration unclear
    → ACTION: Integrate header detection and generation into the main parsing flow: automatically call detect_headers() first, and if no headers are detected, automatically call generate_headers() to create synthetic column names, ensuring the process is transparent to the user.

  FIX 4: code_correctness.edge_case_resilience (current: 75%, 1.1 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Missing some edge case handling
    Expected: Handle 100% of edge cases without crash
    Found: Comprehensive handling present but may not cover all scenarios
    → ACTION: Add explicit handling for these additional edge cases: (1) files with only headers and no data rows, (2) files with inconsistent column counts across rows, (3) extremely large files that might cause memory issues, and (4) files with mixed line endings (\r\n, \n, \r). Include specific error messages for each scenario.

  FIX 5: code_api_design.type_hints (current: 75%, 0.7 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Incomplete type hint coverage
    Expected: 1.0 if complete, 0.5 if partial, 0.0 if none
    Found: Advanced typing present but may be missing on some functions
    → ACTION: Add complete type hints to all helper functions, especially internal functions like _calculate_delimiter_consistency, _analyze_data_type_consistency, and any other functions missing parameter and return type annotations.

  FIX 6: code_api_design.input_flexibility (current: 75%, 0.7 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Missing full input type support
    Expected: 1.0 if multiple input types, 0.5 if one type, 0.0 if unclear
    Found: Advanced input support claimed but implementation may be incomplete
    → ACTION: Ensure the _read_source_content function fully implements support for all declared input types: add explicit handling branches for Path objects, file-like objects (IO[str]), string data, and URLs, with appropriate error messages when input type is not supported.

  FIX 7: code_api_design.docstring (current: 75%, 0.6 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Missing complete docstring with example
    Expected: 1.0 if complete with example, 0.5 if basic, 0.0 if missing
    Found: Comprehensive documentation present but examples may be incomplete
    → ACTION: Enhance the main function's docstring with a complete, runnable code example that demonstrates the full workflow: reading a CSV file, handling missing headers, and accessing the parsed results with error handling.
